chunk_summary: Guidance on energy density and texture safety in complementary feeding for infants aged 6–12 months, emphasizing meal frequency, spoon test consistency, energy-dense additions, and safe texture progression to prevent choking.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of energy density in complementary feeding for infants aged 6–12 months.
Learn how to assess food texture safety using the spoon test and prevent choking hazards.
Identify appropriate meal frequency and nutritious snack guidelines for infants aged 6–12 months.
Explore methods to densify complementary foods with fat and protein sources.
key_facts: WHO recommends 2–3 meals/day for 6–8 months and 3–4 meals/day for 9–11 months, with snacks as needed.
Complementary foods should pass the spoon test: thick enough to sit on a spoon rather than drip.
One teaspoon (5 g) of oil or ghee adds approximately 45 kcal to food.
Thin liquids like dal water or rice water fail the spoon test and may lead to poor weight gain.
Avoid choking hazards such as whole nuts, seeds, grapes, popcorn, and sticky candies; supervise infants while eating.
reference_quote: WHO guidance supports 6–8 months 2–3 meals/day and 9–11 months 3–4 meals/day, with nutritious snacks added as needed and continued breastfeeding.
document_key_topics: Nutritional strategies for infants aged 6–12 months
